anchor: Janesick et al. 2023 Xenium breast TME mapping
published_expectation: High-resolution Xenium can recover breast tumor, immune, stromal, and tissue-architecture signals in targeted panels.
contextgate_observation: Xenium breast rows were eligible for cell-level benchmark contracts, but no real-data setting was routed to context_allowed under strict residual, wrong-context, held-out, leakage, and downgrade gates.
claim_boundary: Spatial architecture can be biologically visible while still failing to show incremental predictive residual utility over expression-only and controls for the tested tasks.

anchor: Ren et al. 2025 subcellular spatial platform benchmarking
published_expectation: Platform, panel, segmentation, and sample/FOV effects can change what signal is observable and reproducible across high-throughput subcellular spatial transcriptomics assays.
contextgate_observation: Failure taxonomy and S25 decomposition expose panel observability, insufficient power, held-out replication, and unresolved downgrade failures; S26 shows zero context_allowed routes across tested operating points.
claim_boundary: The benchmark diagnoses observability and reproducibility limits; it does not infer that tumor communication is absent.

anchor: Fischer et al. 2022, NICHES 2022, and CellNeighborEX 2022
published_expectation: Neighbor-dependent expression and ligand-receptor/pathway proxies can be biologically meaningful and should be tested with explicit spatial context.
contextgate_observation: Positive-control ladders are recovered, wrong-context controls are rejected, and real-data rows are routed to expression-only or abstain when residual context evidence is insufficient.
claim_boundary: ContextGate-Bench supports testing neighbor context rather than assuming it; it is compatible with future context-positive discoveries that pass the same gates.
